text.title: Survey Design Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Survey Design methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Survey MUST have <=12 questions including demographics; longer surveys produce drop-off > 40%.
rule.rationale: Empirical drop-off curve; >12 questions halve completion.

rule.statement: Every question MUST pass bias-lint: no leading, double-barreled, loaded, or assumes-prior-knowledge wording.

rationale.source: Sue Bell, Survey Design Best Practices

rule.statement: Top 2 themes MUST have a mandatory open-text field; quant alone misses the why.
rule.rationale: Closed-only surveys cannot explain unexpected results.

rule.statement: Conclusions per segment require N>=120 responses (90% CI +/-9%); do not publish per-segment claims below this floor.
rule.rationale: Below 120, confidence intervals exceed +/-10%; the conclusion is noise.

rule.statement: Run a pilot N=10 before launch; rewrite any question that >=3 pilots flagged as confusing.
rule.rationale: Pilots catch bad wording that bias-lint cannot.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
